text: Pretty empty at 8ish on a Saturday but from what I can tell Tucson is a pretty quiet place. Anyway, came in with the husband and our sons. Was open seating so we settled down and not even minutes later someone was bringing us menus, high chairs and taking drink orders. They bought out menus and crayons for my boys and were hanging around talking to my sons (people tend to do this often, they are really cute boys). Ordered BBQ wings, the standard burger, my husband got the Tucson burger and I got sliders and corn dogs for my boys. All food was cooked very well, had great flavor. The wings were very good and not too messy, I hate messy wings. The burger was cooked medium well just like I asked. We like all the tables were brought out warm chips and salsa both were very, good. And I'm not a salsa fan but I'm pretty sure I could eat this by the truckload. The manager was very sweet and kind, and honestly people just kept checking on us the whole time and again , she said she was missing her grand babies so she gave my littles a lot of attention but not in a creepy way. Lol. Anyway my kid won't sleep without a cup of milk and this and our hotel is pretty far away so I was dreading driving all over to find milk. We asked the manager and she bought out not one, not two, but three containers of school milk for my son and didn't charge us a bit. They bought out my check without delay and boxes and bags and we had the best experience dining with two kids (especially my 'active' 2 year old) we've ever had. He's never this behaved I may ask the manager to move in with us since she obviously is a toddler whisperer. If in the area, come here I don't think you'll regret it. It's not Ruth Cris but it's good service, good food and a good price point and you can't beat the convenience if you stay in the area. It's connected to the Doubletree btw.

text:
Being a craft beer aficionado, I was really looking forward to Barrio Brewing, but left feeling disappointed. Their beer tasted rather cookie-cutter, with basic styles of Pale Ale, IPA, White IPA, Blonde, Hef, Brown, Amber, et al, and nothing about them that set themselves apart from other craft breweries. The only unique brew they had on tap was the NCAAle, which is actually a pretty good strong ale. 

The food is good. I had the wings, which were meaty and very tasty. I also had their artichoke dip, which I found also quite good. The service was pretty good too, with plenty of bartenders to offering me a new pour.

The inside of Barrio Brewing is actually more like a sports bar. It has a sports bar feel, not so much like a craft brewery feel. The emphasis seems to be on the food and atmosphere, and not at all on the beer. In fact, the place reminds me more of a BJ's Restaurant, with that kind of food, drink, and clientele.

Go there for good service, good food, and a lively atmosphere. If you're into serious microbrews, I think you're going to find Barrio less than impressive.

Steve J
Road Pickle Motorcycle Touring Magazine
http://www.roadpickle.com

name: Jackson Bar + Eatery
categories: American (New), Sandwiches, Bars, Nightlife, Sports Bars, Restaurants
attributes.Ambience: {'touristy': False, 'hipster': False, 'romantic': False, 'divey': False, 'intimate': False, 'trendy': False, 'upscale': False, 'classy': True, 'casual': False}
attributes.GoodForMeal: {'dessert': False, 'latenight': False, 'lunch': True, 'dinner': False, 'brunch': False, 'breakfast': False}
text:
Have been to Jackson twice now and it was twice too many. First time in was the first weekend of service. Two of us stopped in for dinner on a weeknight. Our server was OVERLY friendly. The "are you sisters?" when sitting down with my mom for dinner is trite and off putting. Our server gave us a huge schpiel about how wonderful it was for the owner to be bringing jobs to the community and how amazing it is to be working there. Great, but no need to interrupt our conversation for this. The atmosphere is sterile - nothing hanging on the walls, diner-esqe chairs and tables. No ambiance at all. 

The menu was EXTREMELY limited. Ordered the chefs salad - $16 for wilted greens and some sort of roasted squash. It was over dressed and just not good. The lettuce had been kept too cold and was frozen then thawed. Not worth the money. Then shared the meatloaf sandwich. Thank goodness our server advised us it was served cold. Asked for it to be warmed and it was much more edible. Still - overpriced for a piece of meatloaf. Asked for gravy on the side for our fries - too salty and flavorless. Almost as if it was opened from a can. A salad, a sandwich, and two glasses of Rose should not have cost us $46 plus tip. 

Chalked our first experience up to opening weekend hiccups and returned a few weeks later. We really shouldn't have. 

Wanted to order a margarita and asked the server if they used sour mix in their drinks. Said yes. Then no. Then didn't know. Had to ask the bartender. For the atmosphere the restaurant is going for, the servers should be more educated as to the drink menu. Decided to move around the still limited menu and ordered a burger. Was trying to be good and asked for the burger to be served without a bun and with a side salad instead of fries or potato salad. Was advised by our server that a side salad wasn't an option. Okay. Asked to order a separate side/garden salad. Again, no go. "The Chef can't do that". Can't put lettuce and tomato on a plate with some dressing? Alrighty. Ordered the cole slaw, admitting defeat. Others in my party ordered the truffle burger ($18!?) and the meatloaf sandwich.

Our food arrive and my burger was way under cooked, even for a rare burger. Should have taken a picture - a sad piece of almost raw meat served on a huge plate, thrown on top of some shaved lettuce, tomato and onion. Really? You couldn't do a side salad??? The cole slaw was the same shredded cabbage mix you can buy from Costco and add dressing to. I have the same stuff in my fridge at home. Flavorless dressing included. It was disgusting. The Truffle burger came out extremely over done (also ordered rare) and was sent back. It took almost fifteen minutes for it to reappear cooked to our liking. It is obvious that the truffle comes from a liberal dash of truffle salt and maybe a drizzle of truffle oil on a normal burger. $18 is a joke of a price. In the couple weeks between visits, the bread used on the meatloaf sandwich was changed out. Our first sandwich had really tasty garlic bread. The second time around, it was on two pieces of flavorless white bread. 

We really had high hopes for a neighborhood eatery that was a little nicer than a sports bar for the east side. Unfortunately, Jackson misses the mark completely.

text:
Upon arriving in Tucson, I was told by a friend that this is the best place for brunch...that's a pretty tall order, but I was not disappointed.

SERVICE: They are notorious for their wait time on weekends as I'm sure other yelpers can attest to, but after enjoying a delicious meal here, I can see why! My group of 7 arrived around 11 and were told there was a 2 hour wait!! WHAT?! The place is rather small. It ended up being a little over an hour since some groups before us didn't want to wait. They do have some outside seating as well, and I recommend sitting at the bar if you're only 2 people. It's probably best to come with a group of max 4 people.

The two owners came by our table to say hello and thank us for coming by  and chat for a bit -- that doesn't happen every day! They were genuine and I could tell that they really put their heart into great service, atmosphere and excellent food. 

DRINKS: The mimosas are a must try! Great way to get your brunch time going and get happy :) I had the fuzzy naval and the other girls tried the blueberry one and Madras (guava).  I couldn't really taste much peach flavor in mine, but peach is a mild flavor anyway. I liked it either way - very tasty! 

FOOD: The menu is small but sufficient to find something interesting. After we ordered, one of the owners brought us a sample of their newest brioche french toast to get our opinion on it (see photo). It's not even on the menu yet!! It was a bit tart and not overly sweet.

The ONLY thing I didn't like about the menu is that since I never eat meat in the mornings, that cut out a lot of the options on the menu, as many items have duck, sausage or ham/pork. I had the omelette, which changes every day by the way. Today it had carrot purre, leeks, spinach, tomato slices and cheese, with springs of sprouts on top. It came with hashbrowns on the side. When I first saw the plate I thought it was very small, but it fills you up.  Everyone was speechless when the food arrived -- that's when you know the food is gooood :) Overall the meals were fairly healthy and satisfying for everyone. 

Delicious, I will be back! I'd like to try them out for lunch too.

text:
Kind of a cumbersome set-up, food was not that good, but drink menu is great. 

The place is itself is a nice place to hang-out with a good vibe. The annoying thing is you order your food at the counter, chipotle style, but if you want to look at the full drink menu, you have to go to the bar and ask for that menu, or press the button on your table to get the drinks-- either way you have to pay two separate bills for being in one place.. silly set-up.
The beer menu is great though, with tons of local options. They have a few margarita choices that all sounded interesting and I loved mine. 
However, the food was just not good, especially for what you can get in this town for Mexican food. The tacos and burrito both just tasted dry, to the extent that we didn't even finish the burrito despite being voracious eaters normally. Free chips & salsa bar were good though-- in fact I mainly just ate the chips and salsa since they were better than the food. 

Come check it out for drinks, but I'd go elsewhere for better mexican food

text:
Pros: They seated a group of 6 with almost no notice so that was awesome. It was a Wednesday night but still. It's a small space so I was worried we wouldn't get in. Also, the space is beautiful. 

Cons: The free chips were great but some of our dinner orders were mediocre. For example, I got the mixit salad that came with partly brown avo and dried out beet cubes. My calabacitas quesadilla app looked like something from a lower-end kids menu. Was very dry and flat with hardly anything in it. The beer I ordered was out of stock, but it took a good 20 minutes for her to tell me that. She didn't tell me the beer was out until she was bringing the drinks for the rest of the table. By this point, we had already placed our food orders and no menus were left at the table so I had to go off memory to pick a backup drink. I hate to be such a tough critic but I had heard so many good things and I had higher hopes.

I really love their industrial decor though, so maybe I'll give it a shot at lunchtime since I work nearby.

text: One of the things I miss most from my time living in AZ (aside from the sun) are street tacos. On this trip we sought out the best street tacos in Tucson ... and I think we found them. Fresh, fast, and all on point. I had the Al Pastor and Asada, street and gringo style to try them both ways. So good! I was very impressed. The chips are free with an order of $5 or more, complete with salsa bar. This was my husband's first time with street tacos and he was a fan. We will be back!

text:
My first visit here since being kicked out on New Years Eve when I was like 19.

I heard the food was not bad and so I decided to give it a shot. Our server was friendly and suggested the wings and/or one of their burgers.

Being that I am a wing nut, I ordered wings and a dinner salad. The food comes out in like wicker baskets, it was a little odd. The salad was actually in a small bowl inside one of the baskets, which made it appear larger than it was.

The salad was a run of the mill side salad but the server it with little pieces of toast that are buttery and delicious. 

The wings had very food flavor, I really liked the sauce. They also were cooked well, but they were very small and almost all of them were wings, very few drumettes.

I am going to try this place again. It is definitely an older place, the decor inside is from another time in life but it looks cool. In typical bar fashion, there are like zero windows so it is a bit dark for lunch.

I would recommend trying it out.

text:
3 of us stopped at Maynard's today for lunch. It's a great place for lunch! Here's the food;

I had the veggie sandwich. It came with home made chips. All yummy!

1/2 Cuban and avocado grapefruit salad - superb! Salad was fresh and inventive. Jalapeños on the Cuban was a nice touch. 

1/2 Ham and Brie sandwich and the same salad - also superb. Also delish!

One problem: I wanted a second beer and had to go back to the cashier and wait in line with the others that were deciding what they wanted for lunch. So you wait and wait. This is a business model flaw and really should be modified. 

Even with that, it's worth a visit. I'll be back. Maybe get a bottle of wine instead of beer.

text:
I always have to be literal when I don't care for a place. Sadly, this hooters was a disappointment. They felt quite sterile and NOT a place to make your regular haunt. Beer is always a safe bet so points go there. Food tasted VERY pre-prepared and had chewy texture (wings). I used to go to the one off Tanque Verde in the 90's and it was great then.
If I were to offer advice to this franchise outlet it would be to go back to local sourcing. Other bars and restaurants are doing just that and they are AMAZING.

text: I expected so much more from the awesome reviews this place had to offer.  I took my mom here for her birthday and we chose this location because they were dog friendly and the yelp reviews were great!  We sat outside on the patio because of the dog and the FIRST thing I noticed was one of the staff members visibly getting annoyed because we must have inconvenienced her somehow by coming to spend money where she works.  Thank goodness she was not our server, she had a horrible attitude our whole visit, and she didnt even say a word!  The food was sub par.  We got chips and salsa as an appetizer and the chips were kind of stale and the salsa seems like it came right out of a jar.  Well whatever, for $3 thats ok.  I was really disappointed when they didn't have corn tortillas instead of flour tortillas, so gluten free folks you might want to consider this place.  But dont be too upset because all of their food tasted as if it was previously frozen and packaged, down to the fries and the bun on the burger.  I personally wouldnt go back to this place.  They didnt even have oranges for their Hefeweizen beer.. I mean, come on!  This visit was a bit of a disappointment, and I dont see them staying in business very long.

text:
Within the past month while dining at Maynard's Market, I have discovered a miraculous version of one of your condiments: your ranch dressing. This proclamation may seem hyperbolic, as ranch is often regarded as a rather plebian, low-brow condiment; often served on sad iceberg lettuce, or with fried chicken tenders. However, after enjoying your ranch with a grilled cheese sandwich and french fries (both amazing in their own right), it was as if I had never had ranch dressing before. It was amazing.

 At first, I thought it was an overreaction on my part, but after a few of my friends sampled it, they too agreed it was the best ranch they had ever tried. Not to be under-estimated, these friends were from the Midwest, so they are veritable ranch dressing experts. We were trying to articulate what made the ranch dressing so uniquely wonderful, but the specific ingredient escaped us. It was just so much better than other ranches. 

First, I would like to congratulate your staff in creating such a magnificent condiment. Second, I request that you either 1) bottle this in larger quantities, so it can be shared on a wider scale; or 2) send this recipe, so we can come to understand the source of this ranch's gloriousness. 

I look forward to visiting the Market in the near future. And yes, ranch dressing does deserve a post like this.

I am not giving them a great star rating but I guess thats because I was not a huge fan.
Drinks were really expensive, the bartenders were not too friendly, and the patrons were a bit odd (we were sitting next to a women who we saw doing some not so great things in the bathroom, sniff, sniff).
So why even give three stars well because you could tell that the patrons who did go there were regulars and loved it, I didn't try the food and the atmosphere was nice.  I did like the bouncer he was pretty nice.

So all in all I would go again just to see if it is better than I remember but I wont be rushing to get there.

text:
I went here for lunch on a Saturday afternoon. Barrio is located in an industrial area right alongside the train tracks. The inside had a cool, rustic, industrial look to it. For the time of day,it was dark, but there were small pockets of light that made the inside area visible. 
For an appetizer we got the Beer Sponges a.k.a pretzels. They arrived completely covered in salt and I ended up shaking off  most of the it. Surprisingly, the pretzels were quite tasty underneath. The hot mustard dipping sauce that came with the pretzels was very flavorful, but way too spicy for me. I had to wait a while between the appetizer and the main dish so I famished by the time the rest of the food came out. When my Philly Cheese Steak and fries were delivered, they looked absolutely amazing. Unfortunately, the meat in the sub was extremely dry and ruined what otherwise would have been a great sub. 
Overall, the food was okay but nothing special. I haven't decided if I will come again, but if I do it will probably be just to try the beer.

The duck confit hash was incredible.
Best breakfast out in quite some time.
Everything was perfectly done.

My dad ordered a cinnamon roll to share, was a huge amount of icing, but it was a perfect cream cheese icing, barely sweet, roll had a perfect caramelized top. 

And the service was spot on.   My tea was replaced twice with a new single serving plunge brewer before my current cup was half empty, without asking.

Barely had a spoonful left in my skillet when done, and they still asked if I was done, but only after my utensils were down.

We didn't feel rushed even though the place was super busy and there was a line.

This review is for their happy hour and wine club.  

They have one of the best wine clubs around.  For $275 per year, you get two bottles of wine each month and free wine tastings each Friday (approx 5 tastes).  There is also a discount on their wine dinners.  Wine tastings are also available a la carte for $12.

So each week the wines rotate.  A sampling of what is coming up is:

8/17 - Northern Italy
8/24 - Southern Italy
9/07 - Mexico
9/14 - Washington State
9/21 - Arizona

Food is reasonably priced and very tasty.  Outdoor seating is pleasant next to the rail tracks and they have fans for the hotter nights!

name: Good Oak Bar
categories: Pubs, Bars, Wine Bars, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars
attributes.Ambience: {'touristy': False, 'hipster': True, 'romantic': False, 'divey': False, 'intimate': False, 'trendy': False, 'upscale': False, 'classy': True, 'casual': True}
text:
I have visited the Good Oak Bar twice now and each time was quite happy! I can't say anything about the food because frankly, I didn't realize they even served food until I was ready to leave. 

Drink time...I was quite impressed! The sangria (go cones) blew my mind. I don't usually order sangria because I am not a fan of sweet drinks. The ingredients were so intriguing that I just couldn't resist and had to ask about them. Strawberry, Rhubarb, mint, basil, cucumber, and honey (I may be missing one or two)... I was convinced into trying it out. The bartender promised it wouldn't be too sweet. She was right! This drink was not sweet at all and you could be sure that every ingredient was all natural. I was in love! I also tried the coffee press... not something I would get on a regular basis but it was pretty good and something new and fun to do!

The ambiance was awesome. It seems like a little underground bar that not many know about but it is actually quite popping when you step inside. The bar was at least half full and every booth was taken each time I went in. What was great is that it didn't feel crowded and we were still taken care of very well! The owner happened to be in while we were grabbing a drink and he was really friendly. He stopped by the table, said hello and told us a little about this bar and his others in Tucson and Flagstaff. Before he was heading out he dropped back by just to make sure we were enjoying ourselves. You can't beat that... so we bopped on over to his other restaurant for dinner!

I probably wouldn't make this spot one of my normal hang outs but it is a great place and I will be going back! It would be an awesome date place if you're looking for a drink or two and want to try something new! They have an adorable Photo Booth with fun lips, glasses, bow ties, and more fun sticks to take funny pictures.

text:
I have been going here for several years.  It has gotten much worse over those years.  I like a  nice craft brewery place for lunch and this has the charm of being out of the way in an old warehouse.  The beer is good.  Service is good when you sit at the bar.

But now they serve most of the food in plastic baskets instead of plates.  So the food went from something nice to plain old "bar food."  Perhaps, if I was the owner, I would go for the money too.  But it sure makes eating there not as nice.

What bothers me most is the dreadful men's room.  It is filthy, smelly and overall completely disgusting.  This last Sunday, a toilet had backed up or was leaking so there was almost an inch of water on the floor.  You couldn't even get to the urinal without wading into the liquid.  The odor seemed to drift the the far reaches of the place although I couldn't smell it at the bar.  Maybe I was smelling some other stench while we were playing shuffleboard.

Because we were not sitting at the bar, we were not informed that they were closing at 4:00 pm for a "meeting."  So, of course, we missed last call.

In any case, the terrible bathroom shows how little they care about their customers.  And it makes me wonder how sanitary the kitchen is.

They are always busy (in spite of themselves) so I guess that is why they don't care.

text: I guess this is a case where I just don't get it. The restaurant is wildly popular but I think it's just average. I've been there 3 times. The first time I had the chili relleno which was simply scrambled eggs in an uncooked green pepper with a few tortilla strips on it. The waiter highly recommended the hash browns which came cold and slightly greasy. The next time I had the bacon donut which was tasty but rather tough and a little dry. The third time I had a confection of mascarpone on top of a croissant. But the crossaint wasn't flaky it was tough and the mascarpone didn't quite go. Service was good but the coffee was always lukewarm. So I guess this is a case of more style over substance.

Best Indian buffet in Tucson so far. Came in after hot yoga, on a hot day for some hot Indian food. Oh yeah! We were not disappointed after reading the reviews. 

The buffet station consists of: 2 meat curries (chicken and lamb I think), saag paneer, cauliflower and potato curry (to die for), tandoori chicken, basmati rice, samosas, naan, chickpea curry, 2 salads and freshly made side dips of green chiles and tamarind sauce. 

Didn't manage to try the dessert...was too stuffed.

Will definitely be back once the craving for Indian food comes....which is pretty often :)

text:
Loved the atmosphere when we walked in but the service and the food were not great. There was only one person running the bar and restaurant on a Friday evening. We kind of assumed they had a call out so we tried to give the bartender a break and we were as patient as we could be. An explanation as to the slow, non attentive service would have gone a long way. Maybe even a smile or two. We sat at the end of the bar and the glare on the board made it so we couldn't see what was written. We both got the burger and it was dry, unseasoned and came on what looked to be two different buns. One thing I must say though, is that they have the best fried pickles we have ever had. We only had a couple of drinks because it is definitely pricey. All the wine cocktails were $9/10. 
It is not a place we recommend or would return to.

text:
I've heard tons of good things about Prep & Pastry, so when my fiance and I drove by last Sunday morning, I suggested we stop for brunch.

When we walked in, the hostess said it would be about a 45 minute wait, but she wasn't sure since some people had left without telling her. Looking around the restaurant, there were quiet a few empty tables, and there didn't seem to be too many people waiting, so I was slightly confused. But since we weren't in a rush or grungry (the anger of being overly hungry), we decided to wait.

Luckily, we had only been sitting outside for about 15 minutes when our name was called. The interior is super cute! I really liked the high ceilings and how bright the restaurant was. It was definitely the perfect atmosphere for a lazy Sunday brunch.

After much deliberation, we finally made our decisions and ordered.  I ordered the breakfast poutine and my fiance had the chx + toast (fried chicken strips, rosemary brioche french toast, and a blueberry syrup). My poutine was just okay. It was a solid portion, but kind of bland, and I definitely didn't see any cheese curds. On the other hand, my fiance loved his dish! I thought the different components sounded weird on paper, but once I had a bite, I was sold! The chicken was flavorful, and the rosemary french toast was the perfect savory accompaniment, but I do wish it was a little less soggy. However, I did think the chicken was on the dry side.

Although we didn't have room to sample their pastries, they all looked amazing. I will definitely be trying one on my next visit.

I think overall, I would give Prep & Pastry 3.5 stars, but I'm rounding it up to 4 since I think I would be happier if I got a different dish next time. Also, I don't think the wait would be so bad on most weekends, but the Sunday we went was super windy, so I don't think many people wanted to sit outside.

text: 5 stars, for quality of food, consistency and lively and cool ambiance. Only been disappointed by the food once (sandwich meat tasted dry and was tough) but otherwise the food has been stellar. The have one of the best grilled cheese sandwiches in town, made with brie and pesto, but they excel at even simple breakfast items or the lox option. Their dossants are delicious, every flavor I've had has been YUMMY. Took photos but see there are a million photos of their food already -the presentation of their plates is always excellent. Waiters have to be on their toes to keep up but  are friendly and prompt. It's like a younger, more hip version of Blue Willow. Atmosphere is upbeat, casual, busy. Wish they stayed open longer (especially on weekends) and had more room/tables, the wait times can be very ridiculous at 1 hour to 2 hours for breakfast / brunch on a weekend.

text:
one of my co-workers recently got a promotion into another department and one of our friends decided to send him off with a few drinks and eats while we were all together at this place.

this brewery is located down town off of toole on 16th street (one street before the old 17th street market). they have a lot of space for parking.

apparently, this place fills up quickly on the weekend, especially around happy hour. we got there around 3:45PM to get a large table for our group. the service was alright. our waitress seemed a bit frustrated that our group was slowly trickling in. i think everyone had finally arrived by 6:30PM. 

the food was okay. nothing to write home about. basic bar food. i can't speak about the beer because i don't drink, but everyone else seemed happy with their alcohol selection.

don't know if i'd come back on my own accord, but wouldn't mind if i was invited to another co-worker outing.

Pre-game Arizona-Washington
No Anchovies has it figured out. Order for yourself. Seat yourself. The place was packed but I never felt like I had to wait for anything. If I needed a beer, the bar was quick and accessible. The food was good, quick, and cheap. Winner. TV's everywhere. And I like how they just let Frog n Firken provide the music, based on the close proximity. The menu was simple, but for a pizza place, it had salads, sandwiches, and vegetarian options.

I don't know about all these negative reviews. I've been here several times and had nothing short of a great time. I was here a couple of Sunday's ago for a football game with a friend and I had amazing service.

I will definitely be back here.

text:
While this review is mostly for the yummiest vanilla latte I have had in my life so far, I came here for dinner a few years ago and remember it being very yummy and healthy. Despite being a few years ago it was memorable, but also maybe because it was the first time I ever had grilled cauliflower for dinner. 

Anyway- my husband and best friend and her husband went to downtown tucson for brunch at the cup cafe. The wait was a long 45 mins so we stepped over to Maynard's for a coffee for me and my bffs hubs since we're the only ones that drink coffee, well, unless it's as good as the lattes we ordered here. At first I was disappointed because I ordered an iced vanilla latte and the guy made it hot. I told him I ordered it iced and instead of fixing it, he looked at the receipt and said "it doesn't say iced here" which was annoying, but the flavor made up for it in the end. After one sip of my vanilla latte around the table, my friends husband had the barista add vanilla to his regular latte and soon enough our SOs that don't drink coffee had downed half of ours. Had we not been going to brunch right after, we would have gotten seconds! 

Next time we're in Tucson, we'll definitely be back for vanilla lattes (ordering a large for each of us) and we might even grab food here because it did smell delicious.

text: I was rather excited to try this taco bar, but I have to say, I left disappointment in with some stomach distress to say the least. First off, you go up to a counter to order, and the boy there didn't seem to understand what I was ordering. Then you move to the next person (like at chipotle) and they ask what you're ordering again... Because there's no communication between the team. (That be a great opportunity to upgrade without paying!) The result was, we ended up not getting some of our side orders. But you do pay at the end of the counter. Then we went to sit down and the tables were all dirty, so I grabbed napkins and some water to wipe off our own table quickly since it was the only one available. Then my partner went to grab some salsas. After she set her plate down, I went to grab some salsa and lime and napkins. The salsa bar was messy and in need of cleaning. And I should've heeded the warning of hot taco salsa, cuz the spicy one was so hot my mouth burned for 20 minutes, which surprised me cuz I like hot hot salsa. I had immediate heartburn too... Which I rarely get from spice. The tacos were OK I guess, I make better at home - - nothing to rave about and yes, they were SMALL street tacos. The black beans had a strange spice like rosemary or thyme and were underdone & not very edible. To top all this off, The interior atmosphere was loud crowded and noisy, I can't even tell you if they were playing music because I can barely hear my sweetie sitting across from me. Oh and it was lunchtime, not happy hour. When I got up to get my final refill of water and dump my tray, I finally saw a young man coming around cleaning with a cloth. He left the dirty cloth sitting by the edge of the salsa bar the whole time I was refilling my drink and my partners'. Then I saw him return to pick something up off the floor, and return to grabbing the cloth and wiping around the salsa bar. Later that day when I got home, both my partner and I both had stomach distress. I won't add anything else except I don't think I'm returning to this taco bar, especially because I agree with another review here: we live in a town with way too many good tacos to return to this mediocre taco bar.

text: I love dive bars. The kinds of places that you learn all the regulars and bar tenders names make me feel like home. This bar is set up just right for that. It is small but has all the amenities needed in a good bar: pool table, dart board, bar stools and booze. A friend mentioned wanting to try it and I jumped at the idea because I drive past all the time. I am so glad I did I will be coming back. My friend and I were able to chat comfortably all night but we also made friends with the other people at the bar and all enjoyed our drinks and juke box music in the meantime. If you are looking for a classy place to get a nice glass of wine, this is not the right place. If you are looking for a dark and dirty place to get in a fight go elsewhere. The Rusty Nail had an upbeat every day content feel to it.

name: Good Oak Bar
categories: Pubs, Bars, Wine Bars, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars
attributes.Ambience: {'touristy': False, 'hipster': True, 'romantic': False, 'divey': False, 'intimate': False, 'trendy': False, 'upscale': False, 'classy': True, 'casual': True}
text:
I like this place. It has a lot of wonderful things going for it: 

1. The first and perhaps most important thing for you to know is that they specialize in Arizona beers and wine only. 
2. It is located in prime real estate in downtown Tucson, right in the heart of activity. 
3. I like the concept and the feel of the space. It has wonderful ambiance.
4. The booth seating is spacious and so very comfortable.  

The downfalls? 

1. I prize that this place is all about the Arizona beers and wines, but you won't find a Chardonnay on the menu. 
2. The service is lacking. While they do offer waiter service at the tables, I twice had to go up to the bar to order -- and this wasn't even during rush hour business times. 
3. The space is somewhat small, so it can get short on seating fairly quickly. 

But, really, the most frustrating thing of all was the service. I was actually pleased with my flight of wine and appreciated both the selection and the pours. Still, neither of the bartenders I spoke with were either patient or knowledgeable enough to listen to my interests and give me strong recommendations. 

For a place that specializes in beer and wine, that is a sore, sore mark. 

But, that aside, I still like the space and will likely visit again soon. 

From what I could tell, the bar tends to draw many small groups of people, three or four in a group. It is a great space for socializing; great for casual talks -- I imagine it is a great go to spot for a pre-dinner drink and after dinner conversation. I also imagine this would be a popular spot for people who are in town for business conferences. 

Just a sidenote: The bathroom is an interesting experience. You walk through a set of doors towards the back of the establishment and ed up in a space that opens up to this weird corridor that totally reminded me of a horror movie. The bathroom has an equally eerie feel to it. But it is so, so bright in there so there is likely little changes at all that the Hash Slinging Slasher will grab you while in there.

text:
It's nice to see a local business in the neighborhod. The wine shop has a nice intimate feel, and employees will gladly help you find that prefect vino.
In addition to the wines priced (mostly)
under $15, there are gift items and food for sale.
I went to the shop's recent charity event for a local cat shelter. Half of the proceeds went to the shelter, and they gave away a nice wine fridge filled with wine in addition to other raffle prizes. I was happy to walk away a prize winner with a gift basket.
The patio is a great spot to enjoy the wine tastings the shop has a few times a week.

text:
I am a longtime fan of Claim Jumper but a recent expierence has left me sour !

On my 04/29/12 visit on a Sunday afternoon the atmosphere was nice, the service was great but my fish was incredibly bad.

I ordered the Tilapia Biance and with the first bite I knew it was off.  It tasted like it had been cooked in dirty coppery pasta water.  I ate the shrimp and the side portions but didn't touch the two pieces of fish.

When my waiter, who was very friendly and attentive, took the plate away he commented that I didn't touch the fish, and I said something to the effect of "Ya, the fish was a little off today".  I didn't make a fuss because I was with a party of friends.  I was suprised that he didn't offer an accomodation, as I clearly hadn't eatan a bite of the core portion of my $20.00 Entree.

When I got home in the evening I wrote the Manager and the Claim Jumper guest relations department of my problem and inputted my ticket number and server number, and contact information.

It has been a week now and no one has contacted me to respond to my concern.

I was relatively certain that someone would promptly respond and at the very least provide a gift certificate for a future visit or something.

I will avoid Claim Jumper for the next year or so, there are plenty of other Restaurants in Tucson where I can go on a Sunday afternoon and not be concerned about the quality of the food.

Maybe my expectations are too high ...

text:
Nice divey bar in a strip mall on the far eastside.  Cheap reasonable brews.  Some good variations, but not a lot of craft options.  The main thing is the karaoke here.  Lots of regulars, so you need to be bold and get your requests in as soon as possible.  

Lots of room from groups. I did not try any food, but might on a future visit.  Check the website for karaoke times, but it appeared to be consistent as a 8pm start, but it didn't start for about 15 minutes due to some discussions with the regulars and some newbies like myself just looked around for some direction.

name: Safehouse
categories: Coffee & Tea, Nightlife, Restaurants, Pool Halls, Food
text:
This one's a "I'm glad it's around, but it's not my thing."  After all, the world needs places that counter that sterile, safe, manufactured feel that Starbucks has standardized.  

Safehouse is filled with cigarette smoke, a vintage arcade game, and looks like some kind of awesome desert-ravaged, time-warped, demented 1970s ski lodge.  Ah, yes, that evens things out nicely.

That said, the smoke is just too much for this non-smoker.  The coffee is comparable to other places.  This place is overflowing with atmosphere, though.  If you smoke -- or maybe are shooting a noir film -- I'd definitely recommend it.

text: I love a good breakfast place and Prep & Pastry is definitely one of those "hip" places. I usually prefer more diner style breakfast joints but I gave this a try. I'm very picky with eggs when it comes to restaurants and the best eggs for me personally are from here. The thing I don't enjoy is the fact that I feel as if they try to reinvent foods. The biscuits and gravy are made with duck fat gravy? NEVER do I turn down biscuits and gravy but just saying it has duck fat really throws me off. The food is great, hands down. All of the workers are very laid back which adds a nice touch to the atmosphere. This restaurant is definitely something worth to try if you want something a little different. I'd rather have some down home breakfast but this is good as well.

Great place to hang out and chill with friends away from the crazy college scene in Tucson. An industrial feel with great tables to relax.

This place has great craft brews that you can bring home in a growler if you want! That was key for me, if you like it why not take some home with you?

The prices are bit high for the hipster beers you are used to trying on 4th, but it is well worth it as the alcohol content it higher as well. 

My Faves: sonoran dog, spinach artichoke dip, anything served in a pint glass

text:
Man, sad to say because I really tried to like this place. It's quiet and has a relaxed vibe, I'll give it that. It always seemed a bit over priced and a bit full of itself personality wise with bar tenders who seem disinterested or aloof but I'm forgiving if a place has a cozy feel. 
However, when I order 2 pints of dragoon IPA and hand over a twenty and wait for change and am then informed that they were $10 a pint, that is the line, I'm done ! NO BEER should cost 10 bucks a pint - where the hell am I, Manhattan? I thought I was in Tucson? Also, I've been to Manhattan, many times, and never paid 10 bucks for a pint of even, really good, beer. 
A local bar, serving beer from a local brewery, to local customers can do better than this. And I can do better than this place.

text:
In reality 4.5 stars

I was pleasantly surprised when I came here for brunch. First, the staff takes their Jobs as seriously as a heart surgeon takes performing open heart surgery. And they keep the coffee coming. As does the eater with the glass quart milk bottle left on the table and real sized water glasses.
Interesting dishes and pastries and a liberal use of Duck and gravy in the menu. The strawberry preserves are ethereal  but only 1 slice of bread for side order of toast. The chicken and Toast was great as was the omelette . The pouting was too much.  The thing that brought down the score was the 2 pieces of bacon for a side dish. We like to share an order for the table but since there were more than 2 people......

I love the roadie cup of coffee available and that there is breakfast drinks available sparkling wine is good for weekend. 

And they concentrate on breakfast and lunch

text:
Prep and pastry does everything right. Right from ambiance and decor to the amount of thought put into crafting the menu, the only bad part is that you will have to fight people to get in to eat there.

Every dish is succulent and will leave you wanting more . . . they somehow even manage to turn a simple dish such as an omelette into an unforgettable meal. 

The bloody mary's are a perfect mix of spice and sauce, while their mimosas are refreshing and tangy. This is by far my favorite breakfast trip in Tucson.

text: Excellent ambience and attention to detail. I wish they had put that much thought into their food. Been here several times, always try something new because I've yet to love the food. Upscale Mexican food that consistently misses the mark. Flavors are not there, despite the sophisticated menu. Chips and salsa are tasty, even guacamole is good (although ridiculously small).y husband is from Guadalajara and was excited to try a pork dish that was named after a traditional Southern Mexico dish but resembled nothing like it. The chile relleno, while creative, lacked flavor as most of their food does. The tacos are ok, but again nothing special. I really want to like this place, but would much rather just go to Cafe Poca Cosa.

text:
I really like Sher-e Punjab.  Their dinner is better than the buffet, but the buffet is a great deal!  My only complaint is that the buffet is a little americanized - creamy and mild dishes being the predominant offerings - but what can you expect?  Tucson is predominantly white people without a tongue for spice.  

I've sat in and ordered take-out several times and have always had a pleasant experience.  All of the naan is good, I've had several dishes that I liked and when I've asked for it spicy (8/10), it comes with a good kick.  Once I was adventurous enough to order it as spicy as it came and it cleared my sinuses out.  Score! 

All in all, I would recommend this place and I definitely consider myself a regular.  I tried to branch out and try another place in town and was so disappointed... Sher-e Punjab sets the standard in Tucson!  Just do yourself a favor and get dinner there before trying the buffet.

text: This is the best Indian buffet in Tucson. The Offerings are limited in number, but large in taste. It draws a multicultural crowd, including many who speak Hindi, all of whom has made a conscious choice to go here.  I usually go in early when I have the time, as the restaurant can get crowded during lunchtime but owing to quick turnover the wait is never long. Since it is owned and operated by Sikhs there is always Tandoori chicken, curried beef and either Butter chicken or Chicken Tikka Masala. Always they have a creamed, but not with cheese, spinach dish which is great, oftentimes a chickpea dish, recently a coconut flavored green bean-plus other stuff (amazing) dish and a rotating vegetable dish. They always have basmati rice and naan bread, seasoned salad, rice pudding with fruit and a melon ball. I've gone here for years.

name: Safehouse
categories: Coffee & Tea, Nightlife, Restaurants, Pool Halls, Food
text:
It was a surprise to see they managed to get smoking indoors approved. So the goth/punk/biker smoker crowd is back. I had grown out of my habit and the atmosphere years ago, but last night I decided to try the espresso one last time. Place has not changed.

I think I am too healthy for this place. (LOL) Of course the coffee was crazy strong which they would be proud of. Perhaps my drink had a different flavor when I smoked a pack a day those many years ago, so it wasn't the same. 

Currently I am still suffering the strong side effects of my mega strong espresso (only had a few hours sleep) and the second hand smoke shock to my lungs. Time for me to move on.

Overall, you might enjoy the place if you:

A) Are a indoor and outdoor smoker.
B) Love really really strong espresso.
C) Enjoy Punk, Goth, Metal, Alternative Music.
D) Like a rebellious (sometimes dodgy) crowd.
E) Need Wifi, since you share a blood supply with your computer.

text:
My number one pet peeve at restaurants is when the server is constantly coming up during the meal saying, "how's everything tasting?" or "how are we doin' over here?"  You know....almost every five bites they come up to you after you have just taken a big bite of sandwich?  So the servers at Trident are really friendly and never ever irritating!  They care if you like your food, but they're chill as opposed to some other places where I feel like they really really REALLY want me to know that the taste of my sandwich is the most important thing.
Which by the way, my sandwiches are pretty important.  Because they're so delicious.  We stopped going to Bob Dobb's because my friend always got a burger and it was half the size of the bun.  I like the size of his burger at Trident so this is our new post house work lunch spot.
Also, I went here for drinks one night and it was packed and tons of fun.  
They have several televisions for sports viewing.  I only wish they had outdoor seating.

text:
So after reading previous reviews, I knew this place was going to look exactly like Chipotle but more awesome. Let me tell ya that this place is dangerous becasue I work two blocks away. So its take away with the street menu on the wall, but you know what?? They have alcohol, good looking people, and all the food truck awesomeness you want in a resturant! 
Went there for lunch today and if you want to beat the crowd get there before 11:30am. So many awesome authentic choices of meat and toppings. The prices are pretty average, but when it comes to the add-ons they cant be beat! You can get street corn, churros, and bean sides for $3. Did I mention the churros are fresh and huge! Street cred yo. They also have a full bar with a service button for more at each table. I could not partake during work hours, but I will be back. Speaking of bars, they have a chip and salsa bar, yum! Even raosted peppers to add on for free.
The drink assortment made me smile as I have cravings for pineapple fanta. They carry agua frescas and bottled mexican sodas (or pop where I am from).
Truly a stable, sit down awesome food truck experience in a resturant locale downtown. Make sure to check out all the street food for a pretty fair price. 

Faves: Al Pastor, churros, medium green salsa

text:
i LOVE heading to no anchovies for some grub.  true, i don't do it too often, but i have pretty decent reasons for that.

a.  trying to get anywhere near the u of a  in a car is shitty...especially if parking is required...and especially when you are me.  it takes me an average of five minutes to try to parallel park...what does this tell you?

b.  i am in enough shit with the army for being fat without no anchovies adding to my waistline with their greasy, cheesy awesomeness.

c.  i HATE eating out by myself..HATE IT.  so unless someone is going with me, i either run in for takeout or avoid the place...no way am i gonna sit there ALONE.

now, onto the reasons i LOVE this place (i like to end on a happy note).  there are many, but i will cover the main ones.

a.  the pizza is ROCKIN...big, tasty slices with ingredients that i DIG.  i'm not much of a meat eater, so getting their tomato and motzarella...oh, or the greek one, with feta and artichokes...makes this monkey happy.  you fill up  your sodas yourself...i know this sounds silly, but i like places where i can get my own drink for many reasons...i don't get looked at like i'm crazy for getting it without ice and i can mix diet and regular in the perfect amounts.  i am easy to please.

b.  sitting outside by the u of a is a blast.  omg, i love to watch the obnoxiously beautiful people walk around like the own the world...i just sit back and talk shit.  cuz i'm passive agressive like that.

c.  there is something for everyone, it's pre-made (aka FAST) and it's decently priced for how much you get.

moral of the story...check this place out.  totally worth the time...ESPECIALLY if you can talk someone else into driving.

We had an amazing experience and will be back again. Thanks for reading.

name: Unplugged
categories: Restaurants, Bars, Tapas Bars, Nightlife, Wine Bars
attributes.Ambience: {'romantic': False, 'intimate': False, 'classy': False, 'hipster': False, 'divey': False, 'touristy': False, 'trendy': True, 'upscale': False, 'casual': False}
text: A much needed addition to downtown and Tucson in general! A wine bar with thoughtfully selected wines, spirits and light bites. It's small, cozy and well appointed. It's really a perfect spot for any occasion- happy hour, pre-dinner drink, post dinner wrap up, etc. While small, they've made great use of the space with a few community tables, hight tops and bar seating. They've got a knowledgable staff making sure you pick the perfect wine and the prices are fair. All in all a solid little spot. The service was a little confused and they've got a few kinks to iron out on that end, but surely in a couple of months I'm sure it will be a smooth five-star experience. I plan on being a regular.
